title: Back up and recover the local ledger
preconditions: The owner controls a permitted destination or backup artifact; the live Ledger can be opened or a restore target can be created.
postconditions: The owner has either a verified complete-state backup or a separately validated restored Ledger; every Durable Ledger State type and idempotency outcome is preserved, and the previous authoritative Ledger was not silently overwritten.
main_flow: 1. The agent supplies an Idempotency Identity and requests a point-in-time backup through a public command. 2. Tally produces an access-restricted artifact with format/schema identity and the complete Durable Ledger State. 3. Tally independently verifies compatibility, integrity, every durable entity/history type, idempotency outcomes, per-type counts, relationship/cardinality rules, and exact derived totals without mutating the live Ledger. 4. For recovery, the agent restores and validates the candidate against a separate target. 5. Tally requires explicit owner-authorized replacement before changing the authoritative Ledger. 6. Only a candidate whose normalized complete-state manifest and derived totals match the verified backup may be activated.
alt_flows: 2a. Destination permissions are unsafe or disk is full -> fail without claiming a valid backup

3a. Any Durable Ledger State type, idempotency outcome, per-type count, relationship rule, integrity check, or exact derived total is absent or invalid -> reject the artifact and preserve the live Ledger
4a. Artifact is corrupt, incompatible, incomplete, or uses unavailable key material -> fail before replacement
5a. Replacement was not explicitly authorized -> restore only to a separate target or stop
6a. Candidate normalized complete-state manifest differs from the verified backup -> retain the previous authoritative Ledger
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner wants a recoverable backup, needs to verify one, or needs to recover after device, file, or migration failure.","status":"draft"}
